source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
In the diagram one can see my decision-die in three different positions. What is the probability I get a "YES", when rolling this die once.
<image1>
extraction_notes: answer field is option letter B, mapped to option text

image_dependency: question_text explicitly refers to 'the diagram' and asks about a die shown in three positions
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the visual content is central
the needed information (face labels/orientations of the die) is not present in text alone
multi_step: solving requires combining observations across three die positions rather than reading a single visible fact
the task likely involves inferring which faces correspond across rotations, then counting YES outcomes to compute a probability
multiple-choice format with fractional options suggests a derived result rather than direct extraction
verifiability: answer_text is a precise closed-form value ('1/2'), which is easy to compare against a derived solution
answer_type=short_text and there are 5 explicit choices, enabling straightforward outcome checking
registry_passed=true, question_present=true, answer_present=true, and image asset exists

decision_reasons: 资产完整：题干、答案和主图像均存在，且 registry_passed=true、issues 为空
图像依赖度高且图像资产可用，样本核心语义支撑充分
题目具有较强的多步推理特征，收集价值较高
答案为精确短文本，verifiability_score 高，便于后续核验

normalization_record.normalized_question_text: In the diagram, one can see my decision-die in three different positions. What is the probability I get a "YES" when rolling this die once?
normalization_notes: Removed the image placeholder token from the question text.
Applied light punctuation/spacing normalization without changing the problem meaning.
The question explicitly depends on the diagram showing the die in different positions, so image input is required.
force_requires_image=true is consistent with multimodal processing.
